title: Pleione cumingii
taxonomy: Pleione cumingii is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically belonging to the family Orchidaceae, which is commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places Pleione cumingii alongside other orchid species, many of which are known for their showy and fragrant flowers. The genus Pleione is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. Within the genus Pleione, cumingii is often distinguished from other species based on its unique characteristics and growth habits. Through this classification, Pleione cumingii is positioned alongside other terrestrial orchids native to Asia.

intro: Pleione cumingii, also known as the Pleione orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleione cumingii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Pleione cumingii is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and infusions.
